texto: She spent a great deal of time staring into space, oppressed by the sense that she was waiting. But waiting for what? She did not know. Surely someone would call, someone must be needing her. Yet each day proceeded like the one before. Nothing intense, nothing desperate, ever happened. Time did not move. The home, the city, the nation, and life itself were eternal; still she had a foreboding that one day, without warning and without pity, all the dear, important things would be destroyed.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Evan S. Connell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “She spent a great deal of time staring into space, oppressed by the sense that she was waiting. B…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, change, life-and-death pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e mudanca.

The home, the city, the nation, and life itself were eternal; still she had a foreboding that one day, without warning and without pity, all the dear, important things would be destroyed.
